chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper mentions that ComFASE is an open-source tool and provides a GitHub link in the footnote (https://github.com/RISE-Dependable-Transport-Systems/ComFASE). This constitutes providing working links to public repositories.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation relies entirely on simulation environments: OMNeT++, SUMO, Veins, and Plexe-veins. No physical testbeds are mentioned or used.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: The paper defines the attacks (Delay, DoS) and uses specific parameters (propagation delay modification) to model them. It details the attack injection process in Section III, but it does not provide a single, formal, explicit section titled 'Fault Model' or 'Threat Model' stating precise assumptions like 'up to f Byzantine nodes'; rather, it describes *how* attacks are injected into the communication channel.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper rigorously analyzes trade-offs by quantifying performance metrics (deceleration profiles and collision incidents) across a large number of parameterized experiments (11250 for delay attacks). It explicitly correlates attack parameters (start time, duration, PD value) with the resulting safety outcomes (severe/benign/negligible), providing data-driven analysis of costs.

major_weakness: The paper lacks a dedicated, formal definition section for the Fault or Threat Model, relying instead on procedural descriptions of attack injection.
